solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: exact dict key names '_ctx_stream' and '_ctx_device_index' (test_enter_pushes_to_stack), exact use of PyList_New(0) (test_context_uses_stack), exact use of PyList_Append (test_enter_pushes_to_stack), exact use of PyList_GET_ITEM/PyList_GetItem (test_exit_pops_from_stack), exact variable name 'cur_stream' (test_noop_when_stream_already_current). tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every fail-to-pass test reads the C++ source file as text and checks for string patterns (e.g., checking if 'PyList_New(0)' appears in source, if '_ctx_stream' is in the text, if 'cur_stream' variable name exists). None compile or execute the code. This is pure textual/structural analysis, not behavioral verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub or no-op implementation would not pass these tests. The tests check for presence of specific API calls (PyList_New, PyList_Append, getCurrentStream before setCurrentStream, etc.) and absence of the old buggy pattern (dict.release() assignment, TORCH_CHECK assertion). Simply leaving the file unchanged or writing a trivial stub would fail multiple checks.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All six required behaviors from the instruction (remove assertion, stack-based context, enter pushes, exit pops, dealloc clears, no-op fast path) have corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_reentrance_assertion_removed, test_context_uses_stack, test_enter_pushes_to_stack, test_exit_pops_from_stack, test_dealloc_clears_context, test_noop_when_stream_already_current) and have docstrings explaining what each checks.
